item.title: SA energy minister spruiks fracking potential, but south-east farmers unconvinced
item.description: A decade's worth of gas supply for South Australia is potentially sitting below the Limestone Coast region. But will it ever be extracted?

item.title: Rural women share harrowing IVF stories at parliamentary inquiry
item.description: Farmer and mother of two Sophie Fletcher has endured miscarriages, hours of travel and false hope throughout her IVF journey. She wants to see the system change for rural women.

item.title: Proposed Territory Plan changes to allow 'missing middle' housing on all ACT residential blocks
item.description: Townhouses, terraces and low-rise apartments will be allowed on all ACT residential blocks under a major change to planning rules proposed by the government.

item.title: Shares, start-ups, small businesses and ETFs: What Labor's capital gains tax changes mean in practice
item.description: Labor's capital gains tax changes have set off a firestorm of debate about the implications for small businesses, start-ups, share owners and plenty more. Here's who will really pay more.

item.title: Residents concerned about future of The Palms development in Far North Queensland
item.description: A developer blames the Iran war for his decision to axe a water park and resort project in regional Queensland despite already spending $21 million.

item.title: As Scott Pendlebury broke AFL's games record he also helped restore Collingwood's superpower status
item.description: Scott Pendlebury's record-breaking career has spanned almost five separate Collingwood eras, with each one helping put the club back near the top of the AFL's pecking order.

item.title: Victoria's major party leaders outline election agenda with duelling pledges six months out from poll
item.description: Premier Jacinta Allan and Opposition Leader Jess Wilson deliver their pre-election promises as both parties hold their state conferences.

item.title: Kangaroo Island's little penguin numbers rise after record low in 2024
item.description: Little penguin numbers on Kangaroo Island have rebounded, but estimates show there are only about 700 adult breeders after a harmful algal bloom.

item.title: Woman and toddler die after highway crash in Far North Queensland north of Cairns
item.description: Police said initial investigations indicated a white Mitsubishi Triton ute struck a red Mazda MX-5 before colliding with a white MG3 hatchback.

item.title: Australia's two-party system 'already collapsed' with the rise of One Nation, analyst says
item.description: With the rise of One Nation's popularity and younger voters increasingly turning to the Greens, the traditional two-party system in Australian politics has been dismantled, a former political strategist argues.

item.title: Indigenous artist Tony Albert's optimism is an act of generosity
item.description: Girramay, Yidinji and Kuku-Yalanji artist Tony Albert hopes to "take every item of 'Aboriginalia' in Australia out of circulation".

item.title: A Collingwood champion's achievement has reopened an old wound outside Victoria
item.description: When Scott Pendlebury steps onto the MCG on Saturday afternoon, his name will stand alone at the top of a remarkable list — but the achievement has also reignited a debate about the value of non-Victorian Aussie Rules records.

item.title: UN expert answers Bright primary students' burning climate change questions
item.description: Primary school students in regional Victoria have their climate change questions answered.

item.title: Health Minister denies health cuts, despite asking department to find $700m in cuts
item.description: Health groups fear the impact of hundreds of millions of dollars in cuts expected to be found in the Health Department as part of Treasurer Eric Abetz's state budget operational efficiencies program.

item.title: Some of Australia's most isolated residents live with black mould and broken toilets in government housing
item.description: Bug infestations, walls covered in black mould and broken toilets are just some of the issues plaguing homes in Australia's most isolated communities, where the landlord is the state government.

item.title: Antarctic station discovers its shipping containers floating away on iceberg after blizzard
item.description: Details of how the shipping containers ended up on a giant iceberg have been revealed in one of the more unusual reports tabled at the annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations.

item.title: Police investigate triple fatal road crash in Sans Souci, southern Sydney
item.description: Three people have died in a car crash in Sydney's south, the ABC understands one of the vehicles was likely a rideshare car.

item.title: Albany's abandoned WWII fuel tanks get new life as global graffiti gallery
item.description: Tucked away in bushland on Western Australia's south coast, a hidden World War II site has become a sprawling canvas for street artists from all over the world.

item.title: Bushfires could 'exploit' doormat for entry to your home, fire expert warns
item.description: Preparing for an ember attack could extend your home's bushfire survivability rate to above 90 per cent or even closer to 100 per cent, a fire scientist says.

item.title: 'Life-changing' surf therapy program launched in Queensland to help female veterans
item.description: A Queensland woman has launched a program designed specifically for women veterans to improve their mental health.

item.title: Workforce shortages contribute to psychiatrist's lighter penalty after he sexually harassed nurse
item.description: The only psychiatrist in a regional WA area is given a more lenient sanction for sexually harassing a nurse, partially due to a lack of other mental health specialists in the area.

item.title: Qld man charged by Joint Counter Terrorism Team for alleged 'concerning posts'
item.description: A 48-year-old has been arrested by the state's Joint Counter Terrorism Team over alleged "concerning posts" on social media.

item.title: Vaccination 'not enough' to contain diphtheria spread in remote WA, disease official says
item.description: The director of the Communicable Disease Control Directorate says vaccines help prevent more serious symptoms, but more efforts are needed to tackle the diphtheria outbreak in remote areas.

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX launches upgraded Starship
item.description: Elon Musk's rocket company has launched its new Starship design to enable more frequent Starlink satellite launches and to send future NASA missions to the moon.

item.title: The perfect warm winter salad using crisp-roasted artichokes and grains - ABC News
item.description: Crispy artichokes and textural grain make this salad a satisfying dinner and excellent next-day lunch.

item.title: Shipbuilding woes and tired submarines — why there's more talk of an AUKUS 'plan B'
item.description: Defence industry experts argue Australia should consider how to prevent a naval "capability gap" — or risk being left exposed.

item.title: 'There's more in the tank:' Pendlebury celebrates games record despite Eagles scare
item.description: Collingwood's No.10 enjoys a 10-point win in his record-breaking match as the Magpies hold firm to down the Eagles at the MCG.

item.title: Brent Harvey reflects on losing AFL/VFL games record to Collingwood star Scott Pendlebury
item.description: Brent 'Boomer' Harvey has enjoyed being the AFL/VFL games record holder for the past decade, and says he could have played more if he'd accepted an offer from other clubs to extend his career.

item.title: Take off and landing of SpaceX's Starship V3
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Take of SpaceX's Starship V3
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Darwin man Martin Wiberg dies in hospital months after alleged bashing at Casuarina bus stop
item.description: Friends of Darwin man Martin Wiberg, 48, are remembering him as a "very gentle soul" after he died in hospital this week.

item.title: 'Stay with us': Dragons booed off the field at home ahead of fan day
item.description: St George Illawarra is 0-11 to start the 2026 season after losing to the Warriors in Kogarah, while the Sea Eagles continue their strong form with a hard-fought victory over Gold Coast.

item.title: Auckland FC makes A-League Men history with grand final win over Sydney FC
item.description: Auckland has become the first side from New Zealand to win an A-League Men championship after downing competition heavyweight Sydney 1-0 in a cagey season decider.

item.title: Quiz: Ready to tickle your brain? Guy Montgomery's here with your Sunday spelling quiz
item.description: Know your way around a homonym? Nah, me neither but come in and have a crack at our Sunday quiz anyway.

item.title: Netball's Jamie-Lee Price is frustrated. This is what she wants to see change in her sport
item.description: Frustrated at netball's stagnation, this is what Jamie-Lee Price wants to see happen to keep her sport moving with the times.

item.title: These woodworkers at Melbourne Design Week are building on sustainability
item.description: These artists are carving out a niche in a traditionally male space, using recycled materials and offcuts to create beautiful furniture, and even making music to accompany the work.

item.title: Why Australia still struggles to build bike-friendly cities
item.description: Climate change, the cost-of-living crisis and healthy lifestyle goals should make active transport like cycling a no-brainer. So why does Australia struggle to build a bike culture?

item.title: Five economists share their opinion of Labor's budget and the Coalition's reply
item.description: Five leading economists weigh in on the budget and reply debate, embracing many of the ideas raised while also arguing that "bigger tax reform is needed."

item.title: Visualising the mouse plague infesting parts of Australia
item.description: A mouse plague is invading farms and homes in southern and Western Australia. But what does that really look like?

item.title: Rachel Ward gave up glamour for cows and a not-so-simple country life
item.description: With the country wind in her grey hair, cow dung on her boots and not a skerrick of make-up, former actor Rachel Ward has found her place, sharing her passion for regenerative farming.

item.title: Labor has picked a fight on tax, but not all critics are vested interests
item.description: Labor is banking on younger generations, who are overwhelmingly wage earners, being more interested in owning their own home than pocketing higher profits on a lucky crypto trade.

item.title: Five Italian divers died in a Maldives cave system. Authorities still don't know why they went down there
item.description: The question of why experienced divers would descend to depths of more than 50 metres on a single tank of air — let alone enter a cave while doing so — baffles members of the relatively close-knit deep-sea diving community.

item.title: Holes in Australia's sovereign capability exposed by Iran war supply chain issues
item.description: The Iran war has not only highlighted the vulnerability of global supply chains — it has also exposed the overlooked parts of Australia's manufacturing sector needed in a crisis.

item.title: Regional SA residents say power problems should have been fixed 18 months ago
item.description: After losing hundreds of thousands of dollars due to power outages, Yorke Peninsula businesses say SA Power Networks' $10 million action plan should have come a long time ago.

item.title: Hull City promoted to EPL after beating Middlesbrough in EFL Championship playoff
item.description: Riley McGree's Premier League dream is over and the Socceroos star will hope his World Cup hopes are not also dashed after limping off with a hamstring injury in Middlesbrough's Championship playoff final defeat to Hull City at Wembley.

item.title: Farmers take to the stage to leave stress of the land behind
item.description: Putting their livestock, tractors and drought feeding aside for a night, these farmers are taking to the stage.

item.title: One Nation facing Western Sydney challenge ahead of state election
item.description: The next opportunity to test support for One Nation in Western Sydney will be the state election on March 13. Already, disillusioned Labor voter Silvana Rota is ready to "give them a go".

item.title: Man allegedly armed with gun shot dead by police north of Brisbane
item.description: Queensland Police said the man approached officers and made threats towards them while armed with a firearm, and as a result was shot.

item.title: Early intervention program teaching kids how to navigate grief
item.description: One in 20 Australians under the age of 18 has lost a parent, but families say grief services for children are limited. A national program teaching children how to navigate grief is trying to change that.

item.title: China coal mine explosion kills at least 82, investigation finds multiple safety violations
item.description: An explosion at a coal mine in China that killed at least 82 people was the result of the company involved having committed multiple serious safety violations, an early investigation has found.

item.title: Autumn rain brings boom in birdlife to regional inland South Australia after drought
item.description: Parts of South Australia are preparing for an exceptional year as autumn rain brings an increase in birdlife.

item.title: Endangered river turtles bounce back a year after historic floods.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Endangered Manning River turtle numbers holding steady, a year after record-breaking floods
item.description: Experts are relieved to see the number of endangered Manning River turtles holding steady, a year after devastating flooding in northern New South Wales.

item.title: King Valley prosecco producers prepare for upcoming export label change for Italian wine variety
item.description: Australia is the only country outside Italy that can use the name prosecco, but its export labels will need to change in the coming years.

item.title: WA State Library exhibition sheds fresh light on Albany's pre-colonial history
item.description: Oral histories from Menang Noongar elders are combined with AI renderings of life before colonisation in an exhibition at the WA State Library.

item.title: Independent senator David Pocock won't rule out starting party of independents in response to One Nation
item.description: The independent senator says Australians are frustrated with a political system that "does not work for them".

item.title: Sexual Health Victoria to close Melbourne CBD facility despite concerning STI rates
item.description: The service says government funding has failed to keep pace with increased demand and overhead costs, with rates of sexually transmitted diseases at near-record levels.

item.title: Love story: Teddy needed love, and I knew I could give it to him
item.description: Judith Geppert has an impressive resume of achievements and celebrations, but her capabilities are often questioned. Her dog Teddy, however, never treated her any differently.

item.title: Giants create AFL history with onslaught, Dogs down Dees in classic
item.description: Greater Western Sydney has left reigning premier Brisbane reeling with a record-breaking goal surge, while the Bulldogs get home in a see-sawing contest against the Demons.

item.title: Police officer injured during stolen vehicle crash in Mackay
item.description: The officer was taken to Mackay Hospital in a stable condition with lower leg injury.

item.title: Police say drink driver uninjured despite crashing into pole and over embankment at Aldinga
item.description: SA Police say a woman has lost her licence after crashing into a timber pole and onto a beach in Adelaide while drink driving.

item.title: Man dies after shark attack in Far North Queensland
item.description: A 39-year-old man has died following a shark attack in Far North Queensland.

item.title: One dead, 21 feared trapped under rubble after Philippines building collapse
item.description: Search and rescue operations are underway in the Philippines after a nine-storey building collapsed.

item.title: Air and land search continues for missing 31-year-old man in Arnhem Land
item.description: The NT Police Force says it continues to hold concerns for the welfare of the man, who was last seen early on Friday.

item.title: North Queensland crowd raises record figure for Arrow and MND charity
item.description: North Queensland climbs into the NRL's top six with an impressive 30-18 victory over South Sydney in Townsville, where fans raise thousands of dollars for the Fight MND charity.

item.title: Cottesloe beachfront redevelopment to proceed after securing $15 million from federal and local governments
item.description: An oceanfront redevelopment at Perth's iconic Cottesloe Beach will go ahead with $15 million in funding, but the mayor says more financial assistance is needed from the state government to complete the project.

item.title: At least 24 killed in Pakistan train blast claimed by separatist militants
item.description: A bomb blast has hit a shuttle train carrying Pakistani security personnel and their families in the latest major attack claimed by separatist militants.

item.title: James Duckworth the first winner at French Open after injury retirement
item.description: James Duckworth gets an easy path into the second round of the French Open after his opponent retires midway through the second set.

item.title: How these people's lives have changed since quitting alcohol
item.description: Here's what you had to say about leaving alcohol behind and how it's impacted your health, friendships and lifestyle.

item.title: From sheep mysteries to housing crisis horror, here's what's in cinemas this month
item.description: Plus the rom-com of yore returns in Finding Emily, the farmyard mystery that's been delighting audiences, and a gory Hollywood video game adaptation filmed on the Gold Coast.

item.title: Angus Taylor’s migration cap may not cut migration at all
item.description: Arthur Calwell argued migration was essential to national survival. Today, the Coalition says it should depend on how many homes get built, but the economics are far from straightforward.

item.title: Australians increasingly staying put when it comes to jobs and housing, economic data suggests
item.description: People are less mobile, less dynamic and more risk-averse than we used to be, a series of economic indicators shows.

item.title: Turkish police raid opposition party HQ, fire tear gas at supporters
item.description: The raid marks the end of a three-day stand-off between members of the opposition CHP party and police.

item.title: World-first trial testing whether counterterrorism strategies can prevent domestic homicide
item.description: A world-first research project is aiming to prevent family and intimate partner homicides with similar strategies to those being used to tackle terrorism and lone-actor attacks.

item.title: Iran war's economic shock is reaching Australian wallets and politics
item.description: US President Donald Trump was yesterday touting an end to the war in Iran, but declaring mission accomplished in Washington will not spare Australian consumers, and politicians, from the economic fallout of the conflict.

item.title: Water scientist sounds alarm over $300m Greater South East Irrigation Scheme near Hobart
item.description: A $300 million irrigation scheme will supply 37.2 gigalitres of water to farming areas near Hobart from 2030. A scientists fears the taxpayer-funded scheme could put a major river at risk.

item.title: Donald Trump's deal-making leaves Iran in the driver's seat and Israel more vulnerable
item.description: Israel goes from looking like the new regional hegemon to looking like it is under greater threat now than it has in years, while Iran now has room to rebuild its proxies.

item.title: Tottenham survives relegation battle, Arsenal lifts EPL trophy as Mohamed Salah and Pep Guardiola bid farewell
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur salvages its Premier League status in the nick of time and Arsenal gets its hands on the trophy as Mohamed Salah and Pep Guardiola say their goodbyes.

item.title: Australia has a 'distinctive strength' when it comes to early trading but more knowledge is needed
item.description: Australia has a "distinctive strength" when it comes to early trading, but more knowledge is needed for businesses to fully capitalise on it.

item.title: This week in history quiz, May 25: Will you land a knockout punch?
item.description: Which is older — Alien or Star Wars? And which sea creature did the UK and Iceland go to war over?

item.title: Child sex allegations against Porepunkah gunman Dezi Freeman aired in coronial inquest
item.description: Fresh details into the killings of Senior Constable Vadim de Waart-Hottart and Detective Leading Senior Constable Neal Thompson have been aired in the Coroner's Court.

item.title: Push to rename SA's largest federal seat named after 'contradictory' governor Grey
item.description: Sir George Grey was the former governor of South Australia and is remembered as a "bundle of contradictions" in both Australia and New Zealand, where he played a key role in the Māori wars. But amid calls for the seat that bears his name to be renamed, who was Grey?

item.title: Woman whose rapist wasn't jailed says the fight for justice was still worth it
item.description: The Canberra woman says the justice system is unpredictable for victim-survivors but many aspects are worth it.

item.title: Sydneysiders asked to help rename Bays West precinct, newest waterfront suburb
item.description: In the coming weeks Sydneysiders will be asked to help rename Bays West, while a competition will open to decide the "look and feel" of the future suburb.

item.title: Iran weekly briefing: 'Toll booth' trouble remains as Trump says deal 'largely' done
item.description: The US president says he "won't be rushed" to announce the details, but already critics and Iran are suggesting he has lost the negotiations.

item.title: Stolen Generations survivor urges governments to act in the lead-up to Sorry Day
item.description: At nine years old, Bronwyn was sent to a notorious government-run children's home without her parents' consent. What unfolded next impacted the rest of her life.

item.title: Gippsland farmers push back against Samsung, ZEBRE battery storage on agricultural land
item.description: Companies including Samsung are applying for planning permission to install three battery storage facilities on farmland in eastern Victoria.

item.title: 'Steroid Olympics' gets underway in Las Vegas
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NSW Police announces $1m reward over Susan Kay and Joanne Teterin murders in Newcastle
item.description: The bodies of 37-year-old Joanne Teterin and 32-year-old Susan Kay were found in May 2000, and police concluded they had been bludgeoned to death.

item.title: When is State of Origin I? Kickoff time and how to watch Maroons vs Blues
item.description: Game I of the men's 2026 State of Origin series will be held this Wednesday at Sydney's Stadium Australia. Here's what to know about the match.

item.title: Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk fights back tears at French Open after missile almost hits family home
item.description: Tennis star Marta Kostyuk says she is relieved no-one was injured in the strike, which hit while her mother, sister, great-aunt and more than a dozen others were in the house.

item.title: Man killed in alleged hit-and-run on Perth's Mitchell Freeway, accused driver charged
item.description: A 22-year-old man dies after being struck by a car on the Mitchell Freeway in Perth's north, with the alleged driver facing court charged over the incident after he later went to a police station.

item.title: Travis Head and wife Jess cop social media abuse after on-field incident with India's Virat Kohli
item.description: Australian cricketer Travis Head and his wife Jess Head have their Instagram accounts blasted with vile comments after a series of tense on-field exchanges between Head and Indian star Virat Kohli in an Indian Premier League match.

item.title: Independents confirm they are in talks to form alternative party
item.description: Independent MPs Zali Steggall, Allegra Spender and David Pocock have confirmed they are in conversations about a potential party of independents in response to the rising popularity of One Nation.

item.title: Death of 'drongo': Are Aussie insults and swearwords dying out?
item.description: Classic Australian swearwords and insults are becoming less popular with younger generations. What does losing them mean for our culture?

item.title: NT Health confirms only one possible diphtheria-related death amid outbreak
item.description: The Northern Territory health department is refuting an earlier report that a second person has died with diphtheria in the NT, amid Australia's largest outbreak of the disease since records began.

item.title: How GWS exposed the back half problem threatening Brisbane's AFL flag hopes
item.description: GWS stunned Brisbane and the AFL with a third-quarter goal avalanche in Sydney. But how did they do it and what remains of the Lions' hopes of a premiership threepeat?

item.title: The major sticking points holding up a US-Iran peace deal
item.description: A possible US-Iran agreement is taking shape, but the toughest issues are still unsettled, including reopening the Strait of Hormuz, curbing Tehran's nuclear ambitions, and ending regional fighting.

item.title: James Magnussen flops at Enhanced Games, finishes last in 100m and 50m freestyle
item.description: James Magnussen, wearing a garish golden suit, has finished dead last at the inaugural Enhanced Games event.

item.title: China launches Shenzhou-23 with astronauts aboard
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Football Australia heading for internal changes as staff review reveals 'toxic, chaotic and negative' culture
item.description: Football Australia employees are asked to use three words to describe the culture at the organisation, and the results are damning.

item.title: Dezi Freeman goaded police to 'have a beer' before final shootout, court hears — as it happened
item.description: The details of Dezi Freeman's initial confrontation with police and text messages he sent to his wife after shooting two police officers have been revealed in a hearing.

item.title: Drone vision and laser imaging of Castlemaine Cemetery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian Paralympic great Nikki Ayers announces retirement from rowing
item.description: Paralympic gold medallist Nikki Ayers reflects on a career that saw her face ableism, homophobia and sexism.

item.title: China launches Shenzhou-23 astronauts into space amid new race to Moon
item.description: China has launched three astronauts into space, including one who will stay for a year for a study of the long-term impacts on a human in orbit, ahead of Beijing's goal of a crewed Moon landing by 2030.

item.title: Aboriginal stockman's remains repatriated from SA Museum 60 years after flooding disturbed grave
item.description: An Aboriginal stockman whose remains have been held by the South Australian Museum for six decades will this week be returned to Country in a remote national park.

item.title: Man found dead in Alice Springs home in 'suspicious' circumstances, NT police say
item.description: A 44-year-old man has been found dead in a home in Alice Springs under "suspicious" circumstances, with NT police launching an investigation into the incident.

item.title: Man jailed for fatally amputating man's foot as victim's family reveals distress
item.description: Kalman Tal's loved ones have told of their distress at how the 66-year-old's leg was amputated in a public park. Farm worker John Yalu has today been sentenced for manslaughter.

item.title: Yuendumu residents frustrated with lack of NT government diphtheria messaging
item.description: Residents and service providers in the remote community of Yuendumu, where there are reports of dozens of diphtheria cases, say the NT government has not done enough to keep people safe.

item.title: Mobile GP service and after-hours care to end because of Tasmanian health cuts
item.description: Primary care providers being forced to cut services after losing Tasmanian government funding say the result will be added strain on "pressure cooker" emergency departments, which could see them "explode".

item.title: Dogs banned from Hahndorf cafe three years after it opened welcoming pooches
item.description: Kellie Hunter opened her cafe in the Adelaide Hills in 2023 as a friendly place for dogs and their owners, but the local council has now put a stop to it after one complaint.

item.title: Federal government bails out Rex's multi-million-dollar debt to multiple council airports
item.description: Council-owned airports have been bailed out by the federal government to the tune of $4.8 million, $2 million of which was for NSW alone.

item.title: Budget-friendly mince dishes that will feed the family
item.description: Mix up your mince menu with these versatile and affordable meals for the whole family.

item.title: Motor neurone disease campaigner and former Australian of the Year Neale Daniher dies
item.description: Former Essendon player and Demons coach Neale Daniher has died, aged 65. He was diagnosed with motor neurone disease in 2013 and campaigned to raise awareness of the disease and increase research.

item.title: No body cameras on officers as Dezi Freeman goaded police to 'come and get me' ahead of fatal shooting, inquest hears
item.description: The Coroners Court has heard about Dezi Freeman's final moments before a fatal confrontation at his hideout in Victoria's north-east.

item.title: Andrew Abdo resigns as NRL boss to take up Tennis Australia role, Peter V'landys to take over
item.description: NRL CEO Andrew Abdo causes a shock in the rugby league world by departing mid-season after a highly-successful six-year stint.

item.title: Neale Daniher mourned by AFL community after death due to motor neurone disease
item.description: A great of the game and icon after his retirement, Neale Daniher is being remembered as a fighter and an inspiration after his death.

item.title: Bottled water labelled spring from bores
item.description: A resident of the Perth Hills, where thousands of litres of groundwater are extracted each week and sold as bottled water, has questioned whether it's accurate to label it as 'spring water'.

item.title: ISIS-linked women and children to return to Australia within days
item.description: A group of women and children linked to Islamic State fighters are expected to arrive home within days after purchasing flights to Australia.

item.title: Renewables company ACEREZ destroys Indigenous rock shelter
item.description: ACREZ apologises to traditional owners "without reservation" as the NSW Environment Minister Penny Sharpe expresses her "fury" at the incident.

item.title: Parliamentary committee suspended as senators Bridget McKenzie and Glenn Sterle clash
item.description: A heated exchange prompted a Liberal senator to step in and call point of order on the Labor chair after several insults directed at Nationals senator Bridget McKenzie, before a brief suspension.

item.title: NACC boss Paul Brereton resigns, amid pressure over misconduct, defence ties
item.description: Mr Brereton was found in 2024 to have engaged in officer misconduct in his role as head of the NACC and has since faced pressure over his defence connections.

item.title: State of Origin: Mitchell Moses's hamstring concern prompts NSW Blues to bring Matt Burton into camp
item.description: A hamstring complaint for Mitchell Moses puts a scare through the NSW Blues, with Ethan Strange stepping up and Matt Burton called into camp ahead of State of Origin I.

item.title: Monday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: ASIO's 2024 review of past terror cases didn't extend to prior flag on Bondi attackers
item.description: The head of Australia's domestic intelligence agency reveals it reviewed past terrorism investigations in 2024 which did not extend to a re-examination of the soon to be Bondi gunmen because of a resourcing decision.

item.title: Australia's first female astronaut Katherine Bennell-Pegg invited to International Space Station
item.description: The European Space Agency has formally extended the offer for Australia's Katherine Bennell-Pegg to join a mission that would take her to the International Space Station, but the place is not guaranteed.

item.title: Leaked documents expose how BHP shelved its 'urgent' plans to cut WA emissions
item.description: Hundreds of pages of internal BHP documents reveal how the company sold itself as a climate leader while internally finding reasons to put off action in the Pilbara.

item.title: Iran says 'there is no toll' on the Strait of Hormuz but warns another payment will be required
item.description: While on assignment inside Iran, our reporter asked a foreign ministry official about the regime's plan for the Strait of Hormuz. The spokesperson responded that "there is no toll" but also signalled crossing the strait will not be entirely free.

item.title: At least 20 injured by a substance sprayed at Tokyo shopping centre
item.description: The shopping centre, located in the touristy and upmarket shopping district of Ginza, has been blocked off following the incident.

item.title: Donald Trump calls on Arab nations to recognise Israel as part of Iran war deal
item.description: Donald Trump has boldly tied negotiations to end his war with Iran to a swathe of Arab nations formally establishing diplomatic ties with Israel.

item.title: Ebola outbreak outpacing health response, WHO chief says
item.description: It comes as health facilities in some of the worst-hit regions are attacked amid ongoing violence.

item.title: Rescuers race to reach seven people trapped in Laos cave after flash flooding
item.description: Seven villagers entered the cave in central Laos five days ago looking for gold when heavy rain caused flash flooding, trapping them inside.

item.title: Baby Reindeer creator Richard Gadd returns to TV with new series Half Man
item.description: After Baby Reindeer — about a comedian and his stalker — became a surprise hit, Richard Gadd ignored the pressure to replicate his success, making the show he couldn't stop thinking about.

item.title: Flag quiz, part 2: Line up for our fiendishly hard flag quiz!
item.description: What's the name of the solo star on the Australian flag? And can you tell the difference between the Russian and Dutch flags?

item.title: Here's what experts want you to know about using AI to help with your finances
item.description: "Hallucinations", privacy and other factors to consider if you're turning to artificial intelligence for financial advice.

item.title: Inside the vaults of Australia's legal MDMA and psilocybin magic mushroom trade
item.description: Jess Yugovich is among the Australians trying MDMA-assisted psychotherapy after being diagnosed with PTSD, as highly regulated facilities process the drug for therapeutic use.

item.title: Missing man Jack McLennan's ex-girlfriend feared being 'jumped'
item.description: The former girlfriend of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan says she held fears for her safety because of rumours surrounding his disappearance.

item.title: Flexible work broader than just working from home, advocacy group says
item.description: A bill amending the Fair Work Act 2009 to include the right to work from home is too narrow in its focus and could lead to discrimination, according to submissions in a federal inquiry.

item.title: Rate hikes and tax changes have broken the back of the latest property boom
item.description: Early signs of an Australian housing market correction are now clearly visible, and at the heart of the recent property market weakness is less vendor power.

item.title: BHP has pledged to hit net zero emissions by 2050. Inside the mining giant there are doubts
item.description: The mining giant's delay in the rollout of renewable energy and a fleet of electric trucks and trains in the Pilbara has raised doubts inside the company about it meeting its ambitious climate goal.

item.title: Labor consults on capital gains tax carve-outs as businesses call for 'reset'
item.description: The government is pushing ahead with capital gains tax changes while consulting on limited carve-outs.

item.title: What Xi served Putin, Trump and Albanese at China's state banquets reveals more than any diplomatic statement
item.description: Xi Jinping has learned that every ingredient placed on a state table is a word in a diplomatic sentence.

item.title: Neale Daniher's life as a leader helped him drive the fight against MND
item.description: Neale Daniher showed leadership on and off the field throughout his life, from club captaincy to coaching and as Australian of the Year — but no more so when facing the challenge of an MND diagnosis.

item.title: NACC chief Paul Brereton to face Senate grilling after shock resignation
item.description: National Anti-Corruption Commissioner Paul Brereton will face a grilling by senators at estimates hearings today following his shock resignation on Monday, amid calls for a wider reset of the troubled agency.

item.title: The sporting spectacle of the Enhanced Games was underwhelming but the financial rewards were not
item.description: Despite the drugs and the super suits, the Enhanced Games delivered little more than a smattering of PBs and a single world fastest time for the hefty price of an athlete's hard-earned credibility.

item.title: ISIS-linked woman under exclusion order not returning to Australia with other families
item.description: The majority of the final group of Australians living in a notorious Syrian camp for the families of killed and captured Islamic State militants are expected to arrive in Australia soon. Here's what we know about them.

item.title: Bridgewater Bridge engineer warns of foundation issues that builder disputed during design
item.description: Despite government assurances Hobart's Bridgewater Bridge is safe, there are fresh concerns over its foundation design, with one expert who worked on the project suggesting it could eventually lead to a need to reduce traffic flow or speed limits.

item.title: Moss Vale locals oppose plans for gas-fired power plant to run data centres
item.description: More than 200 people voice their opposition to plans to build one of the country’s largest gas-fired power plants in the NSW Southern Highlands to provide energy for data centres.

item.title: One year after Byron Haddow's Bali death his family demands action
item.description: A year after their 23-year-old son died in Bali in "unclear" circumstances, a Queensland couple have unanswered questions.

item.title: Cricket's next generation of pace knows the toll fast bowling can take
item.description: Cricket has few more glorious sights than a fast bowler terrorising a top-class batting order, but becoming a speedster is not without its pains, as the nation's next generation is finding out.

item.title: Woman charged with murder after child found dead in Lowood
item.description: Emergency services were called to a Lowood residence just before 11:10pm on Monday.

item.title: Former Berrivale workers remember Riverland food manufacturing history
item.description: Sixteen years after workers clocked off for the final time, Berrivale workers reunited to remember the cannery and preserving factory that employed generations of locals.

item.title: Thanasi Kokkinakis, Alex de Minaur and Daria Kasatkina advance to French Open second round
item.description: Thanasi Kokkinakis has roared back from the brink of defeat after four-and-a-quarter hours in sweltering Paris heat to launch another trademark five-set comeback at the French Open.

item.title: Bringing Them Home report inaction a 'failure', former commissioner says
item.description: Mick Dodson heard "soul-destroying" testimony from more than 500 First Nations people during a landmark inquiry. He says that 29 years later, it's time for action.

item.title: Auditor-general criticises Tasmania's 'digital transformation' human resources program
item.description: The total cost of the digital transformation program is now estimated to more than double to $119 million, the auditor-general warns.

item.title: Live: Activists protest about treatment of Gaza flotilla at Parliament House demonstration
item.description: Activists stage a protest at Parliament House in Canberra against Israel's treatment of Gaza flotilla activists. Follow live.

item.title: Live: Australian stocks fall as US strikes dampen investor sentiment
item.description: The local share market is in the red, while shares in stock exchange operator ASX Ltd have tumbled on a trading update. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Teen star Eva Carneiro becomes Super Netball's youngest-ever player
item.description: The Giants team is flailing at the bottom of the ladder, winless after 11 rounds and the likely recipient of this year's wooden spoon. But their brand new debutant Eva Carneiro has brought some much needed excitement for fans, as the teenager breaks the record for the youngest-ever player in Super Netball.

item.title: Vandals saw off road signs in protest against reduced speed limits on Victorian roads
item.description: Police believe changes to speed limits on country roads in Victoria have led to vandalism including new road signs being cut down.

item.title: Drones malfunction during Vivid Sydney show
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Vivid Sydney drone shows cancelled for now after malfunction saw 89 drones fall into the harbour
item.description: The incident occurred not long into Monday's 7:30pm "star-bound" show when almost 90 drones began falling out of formation and into the harbour around Cockle Bay.

item.title: Essendon part ways with Brad Scott as AFL head coach
item.description: Essendon sacks head coach Brad Scott as the Bombers languish at the bottom of the AFL ladder.

item.title: A century on from Miles Davis's birth, his legacy still shapes jazz
item.description: From Illinois to New York to Australia, legendary trumpeter Miles Davis helped shape the direction of jazz with albums like Birth of the Cool and Kind of Blue.

item.title: 'Inhumane' NDIS home in Broome subject to allegations of neglect
item.description: Former workers at a disability home in Western Australia's far north raise allegations of neglect, including medical mismanagement, locked fridges and a child being placed at risk.

item.title: Riley McGree to miss FIFA World Cup for Socceroos with injury
item.description: The Socceroos have been dealt a massive injury blow ahead of next month's FIFA World Cup, with midfielder Riley McGree ruled out of the tournament.

item.title: Power prices to fall for most customers, with bigger drops for businesses
item.description: Surging levels of renewable energy and better reliability from coal-fired generators are set to give consumers a break, with benchmark power prices to fall up to 10 per cent for consumers and more for small businesses.

item.title: Mitchell Moses ruled out of State of Origin Game I for NSW Blues
item.description: The NSW Blues have confirmed five-eighth Mitchell Moses will miss Wednesday night's State of Origin opener due to a hamstring injury.

item.title: 'Morale' boost for Brokenwood Wines after 2026 NSW President's Medal win
item.description: A winery squeezing value out of every part of the vine has received the country's top agricultural gong at a difficult time for the wine industry.

item.title: Israeli Opposition Leader Yair Lapid calls for investigation into alleged abuse of Gaza flotilla activists
item.description: One of Israel's most prominent opposition politicians has called for an investigation into the alleged abuse of activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla, which was intercepted by the Israeli military in the Mediterranean last week.

item.title: Shopping trolleys pollute creek in Rockhampton
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rockhampton environmentalists call for action as supermarket trolleys pollute local waterways
item.description: A "tragedy of the trolleys" is unfolding in Rockhampton, where a lack of governance over waterways is creating an environmental hazard.

item.title: Grain growers adapt as low May rainfall highlights a drying autumn trend
item.description: A downward trend in autumn rainfall has WA farmers adapting to early seeding, but hoping this week's forecast cold front will provide a much-needed break to the dry spell.

item.title: Government sets up fight with second-largest gas exporter over domestic reservation plan
item.description: The draft framework outlines a requirement for gas producers to supply 20 per cent of their export to the domestic market, which the gas lobby argues will "undermine" the industry.

item.title: Federal department says Tasmania failed to deliver 'sensible or useable' Nuyina icebreaker refuelling solution
item.description: A federal department says the Tasmanian government's proposed interim solution to a costly refuelling saga affecting Australia's Antarctic icebreaker is "far from" acceptable.

item.title: Two arrested in Vietnam over shooting death of Sydney gangster Lorenzo Lemalu
item.description: Authorities in Vietnam have arrested two men after Sydney crime figure Lorenzo Lemalu was shot dead in Ho Chi Minh City last week.

item.title: US Central Command says it has launched strikes against Iranian boats and missile launch sites
item.description: US Centcom says the strikes were designed "to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces".

item.title: Royal commissioner issues warning over harassment of witnesses, officer defends patrols at Bondi Hanukkah event
item.description: Four officers were present at the event when the shooting started, with Jewish security organisation Community Security Group NSW denied its request to have police on patrol for the entirety of the event.

item.title: Pipes from former church organ stolen in Adelaide's eastern suburbs, SA Police investigates
item.description: The pipe theft comes days after a bronze statue was stolen from the centre of a fountain in Adelaide's inner north-eastern suburbs.

item.title: 89 drones fall out of formation at Vivid Sydney show
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: SA Police return to Yunta as part of Gus Lamont investigation
item.description: Police have returned to Yunta in South Australia's far north as part of their investigation into the disappearance of four-year-old boy Gus Lamont.

item.title: Roger Tuivasa-Sheck to miss rest of 2026 NRL season after ACL injury
item.description: Warriors club legend Roger Tuivasa-Sheck appears to have played his last NRL game after confirming he suffered an anterior cruciate ligament injury in round 12.

item.title: Liddell Power Station demolition underway after 50 years of operation
item.description: The demolition of the Liddell Power Station, which powered homes and businesses in NSW for more than 50 years, is underway.

item.title: NT diphtheria cases falling as 10,000 vaccines rolled out, test confirms man's death related
item.description: The Northern Territory government says the rate of new diphtheria cases in the territory has fallen from more than 20 per week to less than 10 after 10,000 Territorians stepped up to be immunised against the ongoing outbreak.

item.title: Stuart Highway closed after major crash involving road train and car in SA outback
item.description: A major outback highway in South Australia's north is closed in both directions after a crash involving a road train and a vehicle early this morning.

item.title: Ashley Youth Detention Centre slated to become minimum-security adult prison farm
item.description: The Tasmanian government says Ashley Youth Detention Centre could become a minimum-security adult prison and "agriculture-based prison rehab and training facility".

item.title: Premier Roger Cook announces push for weapons manufacturing hub in Western Australia
item.description: Missiles for use in the Australian military could soon be made in Western Australia with the government seeking to establish a local weapons manufacturing hub, the third state to do so.

item.title: When moving forward means being the cycle-breaker in a family
item.description: Motherhood has a way of making family patterns impossible to ignore. Suddenly you start asking questions you never asked before.

item.title: Teen who robbed tourists after escaping detention centre avoids further time behind bars
item.description: One of the teenagers involved in a group escape from the Ashley Youth Detention Centre in December is sentenced for his involvement in the armed robbery of two tourists.

item.title: Liddell Power Station in NSW Hunter Valley demolised
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Crew completes 13-hour mission to rescue injured fisherman off central Queensland coast
item.description: The pilot says the remote retrieval of the fisherman was the most challenging rescue of his career.

item.title: Why rugby league needs State of Origin to save a lost NRL season
item.description: Players, coaches and fans are disillusioned with the rule changes that have come to define this NRL season but ahead of Origin I can the interstate series put a broken season back together?

item.title: Sonny Rollins, saxophonist and restless genius of jazz, dies at 95
item.description: Rollins, one of the greatest saxophonists of all time, played with everyone from Miles Davis to The Rolling Stones.

item.title: How a broken leg led to a 13-hour helicopter rescue in rough seas.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Gold Coast council seeking partners to build arts precinct for screen, tech and gaming
item.description: The council wants to repurpose an existing depot into a major creative arts precinct that it says will generate $130 million annually and create 1,000 jobs.

item.title: Brinkworth family loses appeal over judgement that father was responsible for Blackford bushfire
item.description: A court judgement that found pastoralist Tom Brinkworth liable for a 2021 bushfire, five months after he died, has been upheld after an appeal by Mr Brinkworth's family.

item.title: What are Trump's Abraham Accords, and will other countries join?
item.description: As the US president pushes for a deal with Iran, he is reviving one of the signature foreign policy projects of his first term: the Abraham Accords. Here's what to know about the series of agreements, and whether Arab countries will answer his call to formally establish diplomatic ties with Israel.

item.title: Two men arrested by Victoria Police's Dezi Freeman taskforce
item.description: Detectives investigating the movements of police killer Dezi Freeman have arrested two men in Victoria's north-east.

item.title: A drone gets up close to the demolition of two chimney towers at the Liddell Power Station
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Kumanjayi White's family told no charges to be laid against officers involved in death in police custody
item.description: The family of the 24-year-old man who died while in police custody at an Alice Springs supermarket last year has released a statement after hearing no charges would be laid over his death.

item.title: Port Fairy swimming pool to be demolished following unanimous council vote
item.description: The pool's shuttering has been met by community uproar, with some locals rallying to protest the closure and calling for it to be reopened.
